title: Five things you can feel, four you can hear
subtitle: A sensory grounding meditation for bringing the mind out of time-travel and into the present moment before sleep.
excerpt: A short sensory grounding meditation for Day 8 of the SLEEP program. Five physical sensations, four sounds. Not a complete 5-4-3-2-1, just enough to anchor the mind to now.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The sensory grounding structure draws on the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique widely used in anxiety and trauma therapies.

content.text: A short grounding meditation for Day 8 of the SLEEP program. The practice uses touch and sound to break time-travel thinking (the mind running ahead to tomorrow, looping back over today). It brings your attention back to the room you are in.
Lie down. Close your eyes. Take one slow breath.

Move your attention slowly through the body and name five physical sensations. The weight of the pillow under your head. The temperature of the air on your face. The contact between your back and the mattress. The feel of the duvet against your hands. Your breath moving in your chest.
Don't rush. Let each one register before moving to the next.
Let your hearing expand. Name four sounds in the room or outside it. Your own breathing. The hum of the building. A car passing. Silence itself.
Notice where you are. This room. This bed. This breath. This moment.

Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The sensory grounding structure draws on the 5-4-3-2-1 technique used in anxiety and trauma therapies.
